papers.title: Using thematic analysis in psychology
papers.takeaway: The original six-phase TA paper. The 'reflexive TA' framing — and the formulation of themes as 'patterns of shared meaning organized around a central concept,' not clusters — comes from the 2019–2021 refinements (next entry).

papers.title: Reflecting on reflexive thematic analysis
papers.venue: Qualitative Research in Sport, Exercise and Health 11(4): 589–597
papers.takeaway: Argues against inter-rater reliability for reflexive TA — subjectivity is a generative resource, not bias to eliminate.

papers.title: GATOS — LLM-based human-LLM collaborative inductive coding
papers.takeaway: Embeds clusters, kNN against existing codebook (k=5), create-or-merge. Synthetic ground-truth validation. Our closest prior art.

papers.title: CHALET — Human-LLM collaboration for qualitative analysis
papers.takeaway: Uses irreconcilable human–LLM disagreement as a source of grounded conceptual discovery beyond the initial codebook.

papers.title: AbductivAI — AI as a co-researcher
papers.takeaway: 8-phase abductive model with a 'Phase 5.5 CoT-Review loop' for divergence detection. Uses Cohen's κ — sits closer to codebook TA than reflexive TA.

papers.title: Thematic-LM
papers.takeaway: Coder / aggregator / reviewer / theme-coder agents, identity-perspective coders, GPT-4o. Demonstrates that distinct personas increase divergence.

papers.title: TAMA — clinical thematic analysis with multi-agents
papers.takeaway: Coder–reviewer–refiner. Reports Coverage / Actionability / Distinctiveness / Relevance metrics and ~99% time reduction.

papers.title: DeTAILS — six-phase RTA with codebook-as-boundary-object
papers.takeaway: 18-researcher study on Reddit data. Confirms Braun & Clarke's six phases map cleanly to LLM calls — and that 'reflexivity' degenerates to verifying AI output without intervention.

papers.title: Chalmers SE study — rubric-based RTA evaluation
papers.takeaway: Blind expert evaluators preferred LLM codes 61% of the time but flagged fragmentation and missed latent interpretations. The template for our exit-gate evaluation.

papers.title: Inductive Thematic Saturation (ITS)
papers.takeaway: Saturation as the divergence between cumulative-total and cumulative-unique code curves. A soft signal, not a hard stop.

papers.title: Temperature and Persona in multi-agent LLM coding
papers.takeaway: 77,000+ coding decisions across six open-source LLMs: personas delayed consensus in 4 of 6 models with minimal accuracy gain. Multi-agent is task-conditional.

papers.title: Abductive analysis in theory and practice
papers.takeaway: Abduction is a 'creative inferential process aimed at producing new hypotheses … based on surprising research evidence.' Our theoretical anchor for the surprise-detector (post-v0).
